title: asclepias asperula
intro: Asclepias asperula, also known as the Antelope Horn Milkweed, is a perennial plant native to North America. It belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve digestive issues. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health problems, including indigestion, nausea, and respiratory issues. Asclepias asperula can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile herbal remedy for those seeking natural relief.
